instructions.workspaceIdentification: FIRST STEP of every operation: identify which workspace (company) the user is talking about from conversation context. Use contextClues in shared-agent-contract.json. If ambiguous, ASK before proceeding. NEVER assume any workspace as default.
instructions.stateAccess: Use gh CLI or GitHub API to read/write state on autopilot-state branch
instructions.lockProtocol: Acquire lock via state file creation, check for existing lock first. Always release in finally/cleanup stage.
instructions.sessionGuard: Before any state-changing operation, acquire session lock. If another agent holds the lock, ABORT - never force override.
instructions.releaseProtocol: Read workspace.json for repo config, never hardcode repos or paths. Use apply-source-change.yml for code modifications.
instructions.handoffProtocol: Create handoff JSON in workspace handoffs/ dir, set toAgent field. Accept handoffs from Claude or other agents.
instructions.auditProtocol: Append audit entry after every state-mutating operation. Include operation, timestamp, runId, component, and result.
instructions.errorHandling: Always validate API responses. Use jq with fallbacks. Log errors before continuing.
instructions.sourceCodeChanges: Use apply-source-change.yml pipeline. Content must be base64-encoded between jobs. Always fix pre-existing lint errors before pushing.
instructions.monitoring: After triggering any workflow, monitor all stages until completion. Report progress and final status.
instructions.autonomousOperation: Can independently execute deploy cycles, monitor CI, diagnose failures, and trigger fixes without human intervention.

triggerFiles.description: Workflows can be triggered by modifying trigger/*.json files
triggerFiles.pattern: Bump the 'run' field to trigger the associated workflow

sessionMemory.protocol: MUST read at session start, MUST update at session end
sessionMemory.purpose: Cumulative memory of decisions, patterns, lessons learned across sessions

deploy: 1. Read trigger/source-change.json for current run and version
2. Prepare patches in patches/ if code changes needed
3. Update trigger with new version, changes array, bump run
6. Commit all changes, push, create PR
7. Wait for auto-merge or manual merge
9. Monitor corporate CI (Esteira de Build NPM)
10. Verify CAP promotion and state update
ciDiagnose: 1. Read current CI status via ci-status-check workflow
2. If failed, trigger ci-diagnose.yml to fetch logs
3. Analyze logs for known error patterns
4. Apply fix via fix-corporate-ci.yml or new source-change
